faqs.question: What is a spice and where does it come from?
faqs.answer: A spice is a part of a plant used to add flavour, colour, or smell to food. Spices come from different parts of plants - turmeric comes from the root, cinnamon from bark, cloves from flower buds, cumin and mustard from seeds, and curry leaves from leaves. India grows a huge variety of spices because of its different climates.

faqs.question: Why is black pepper called the 'King of Spices'?
faqs.answer: Black pepper is called the King of Spices because it is the most widely used spice in the world and was once the most valuable. Long ago, it was so precious that it was sometimes used as money! It grows mainly in Kerala, and traders from far-off countries came to India just to buy it.

faqs.question: What is the difference between whole spices and ground spices?
faqs.answer: Whole spices are spices in their natural form - like whole cumin seeds, cinnamon sticks, or cardamom pods. Ground spices are these same spices crushed into a fine powder. Whole spices keep their flavour for longer, while ground spices lose their taste faster but mix into food more easily.

faqs.question: Why does food taste different in different parts of India?
faqs.answer: Food tastes different because each region has different climate, soil, and crops. Coastal areas like Kerala use coconut because coconut trees grow well there. Rajasthan is hot and dry, so people use strong spices to preserve food. Punjab grows lots of wheat and has dairy, so their food uses roti and butter. Local spices and ingredients give each region its special flavour.

faqs.question: What is garam masala and is it the same everywhere?
faqs.answer: Garam masala is a blend of ground spices commonly used in Indian cooking. It usually contains cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, cumin, and black pepper. But the exact mix is different in every region and even every family! Each household may have its own special recipe passed down from grandparents.

faqs.question: Why did traders from Europe come to India for spices?
faqs.answer: European traders came to India because Indian spices like pepper, cloves, and cinnamon were extremely rare and expensive in Europe. Spices were used to flavour and preserve food, and they did not grow in cold European climates. The desire for spices led to the discovery of new sea routes and changed world history.

faqs.question: Which Indian state is most famous for spices?
faqs.answer: Kerala, in South India, is often called the 'Spice Garden of India'. It grows black pepper, cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, and nutmeg. The warm, rainy climate of Kerala is perfect for growing these spices. However, other states also grow important spices - Kashmir grows saffron, and Rajasthan grows cumin and coriander.

faqs.question: Can spices be used as medicine?
faqs.answer: Yes! Many Indian spices have been used as traditional medicine for centuries. Turmeric (haldi) is an antiseptic and helps heal wounds. Ginger (adrak) helps with colds and coughs. Cloves (laung) ease toothaches. Ajwain helps with stomach aches. Even today, many people drink haldi doodh or ginger chai when feeling unwell.

faqs.question: What is a masala dabba?
faqs.answer: A masala dabba is a round, metal spice box that you will find in almost every Indian kitchen. It has a lid and contains 7 small round cups inside, each holding a different everyday spice like turmeric, chilli powder, cumin, mustard seeds, coriander, garam masala, and salt. It keeps all the important spices together and within easy reach while cooking.

faqs.question: Why is saffron so expensive?
faqs.answer: Saffron (kesar) is the most expensive spice because it takes a huge amount of work to collect. The saffron threads are the tiny stigma of the crocus flower, and each flower produces only 3 threads! It takes about 75,000 flowers to make just 500 grams of saffron. In India, saffron is grown mainly in Kashmir.
